sections.label: Section 652b
sections.translation: Tell me , pray .

sections.label: Section 653
sections.original: τίς ἄλλος ἀντὶ τουτουὶ τοῦ δακτύλου ;
sections.translation: What else but this finger ? Formerly , indeed , when I was yet a boy , this here !

sections.label: Section 655
sections.translation: You are boorish and stupid .

sections.label: Section 655b
sections.translation: For I do not desire , you wretch , to learn any of these things .

sections.label: Section 656b
sections.translation: What then ?

sections.label: Section 657
sections.translation: That , that , the most unjust cause .

sections.label: Section 658
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἕτερα δεῖ σε πρότερα τούτου μανθάνειν ,
sections.translation: But you must learn other things before these ; namely , what quadrupeds are properly masculine .

sections.label: Section 660
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οἶδʼ ἔγωγε τἄρρενʼ , εἰ μὴ μαίνομαι ·
sections.translation: I know the males , if I am not mad :— κριὸς , τράγος , ταῦρος , κύων , ἀλεκτρυών .

sections.label: Section 662
sections.original: ὁρᾷς ὃ πάσχεις ; τήν τε θήλειαν καλεῖς
sections.translation: Do you see what you are doing ? You are calling both the female and the male ἀλεκτρυὼν in the same way .

sections.label: Section 664
sections.translation: How , pray ? Come , tell me .

sections.label: Section 664b
sections.translation: How ? The one with you is ἀλεκτρὺων , and the other is ἀλεκτρὺων also .

sections.label: Section 665
sections.original: νὴ τὸν Ποσειδῶ . νῦν δὲ πῶς με χρὴ καλεῖν ;
sections.translation: Yea , by Neptune ! How now ought I to call them ?

sections.label: Section 666
sections.translation: The one ἀλεκτρύαινα and the other ἀλέκτωρ .

sections.label: Section 667
sections.original: ἀλεκτρύαιναν ; εὖ γε νὴ τὸν Ἀέρα ·
sections.translation: Ἀλεκτρύαινα ? Capital , by the Air ! So that , in return for this lesson alone , I will fill your kardopos full of barley-meal on all sides .

sections.label: Section 670
sections.original: ἰδοὺ μάλʼ αὖθις τοῦθʼ ἕτερον · τὴν κάρδοπον
sections.translation: See ! See ! There again is another blunder ! You make κάρδοπος , which is feminine , to be masculine .

sections.label: Section 671b
sections.translation: In what way do I make κάρδοπος masculine ?

sections.label: Section 672b
sections.translation: Most assuredly ; just as if you were to say Κλεώνυμος .

sections.label: Section 673b
sections.translation: How pray ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 674
sections.translation: Κάρδοπος with you is tantamount to Κλεώνυμος .

sections.label: Section 675
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ὦγάθʼ οὐδʼ ἦν κάρδοπος Κλεωνύμῳ ,
sections.translation: Good sir , Cleonymus had no kneading-trough , but kneaded his bread in a round mortar . How ought I to call it henceforth ?

sections.label: Section 677b
sections.translation: How ? Call it καρδόπη , as you call Σωστράτη .

sections.label: Section 679
sections.translation: Καρδόπη in the feminine ?

sections.label: Section 679b
sections.translation: For so you speak it rightly .

sections.label: Section 680
sections.original: ἐκεῖνο δʼ ἦν ἄν , καρδόπη , Κλεωνύμη .
sections.translation: But that would make it καρδόπη , Κλεωνύμη .

sections.label: Section 681
sections.original: ἔτι δή γε περὶ τῶν ὀνομάτων μαθεῖν σε δεῖ ,
sections.translation: You must learn one thing more about names , what are masculine and what of them are feminine .

sections.label: Section 683
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οἶδʼ ἔγωγʼ ἃ θήλεʼ ἐστίν .
sections.translation: I know what are female .

sections.label: Section 683b
sections.translation: Tell me , pray .

sections.label: Section 684
sections.translation: Lysilla , Philinna , Clitagora , Demetria .

sections.label: Section 685
sections.translation: What names are masculine ?

sections.label: Section 685b
sections.translation: Thousands ; Philoxenus , Melesias , Amynias .

sections.label: Section 687
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ὦ πόνηρε ταῦτά γʼ ἐστʼ οὐκ ἄρρενα .
sections.translation: But , you wretch ! These are not masculine .

sections.label: Section 688
sections.translation: Are they not males with you ?

sections.label: Section 688b
sections.translation: By no means ; for how would you call Amynias , if you met him ?

sections.label: Section 690
sections.original: ὅπως ἄν ; ὡδί , δεῦρο δεῦρʼ Ἀμυνία .
sections.translation: How would I call ? Thus : Come hither , come hither Amynia !

sections.label: Section 691
sections.translation: Do you see ? You call Amynias a woman .

sections.label: Section 692
sections.translation: Is it not then with justice , who does not serve in the army ? But why should I learn these things , that we all know ?

sections.label: Section 694
sections.original: οὐδὲν μὰ Δίʼ ἀλλὰ κατακλινεὶς δευρὶ —
sections.translation: It is no use , by Jupiter ! Having reclined yourself down here-

sections.label: Section 694b
sections.translation: What must I do ?

sections.label: Section 695
sections.translation: Think out some of your own affairs .

sections.label: Section 696
sections.original: μὴ δῆθʼ ἱκετεύω σʼ ἐνγεταῦθʼ · ἀλλʼ εἴ γε χρή ,
sections.translation: Not here , pray , I beseech you ; but , if I must , suffer me to excogitate these very things on the ground .

sections.label: Section 698
sections.translation: There is no other way .

sections.label: Section 698b
sections.translation: Unfortunate man that I am ! What a penalty shall I this day pay to the bugs !

sections.label: Section 700
sections.original: φρόντιζε δὴ καὶ διάθρει πάντα τρόπον τε σαυτὸν
sections.translation: Now meditate and examine closely ; and roll yourself about in every way , having wrapped yourself up ; and quickly , when you fall into a difficulty , spring to another

sections.label: Section 705
sections.original: νόημα φρενός · ὕπνος δʼ ἀπέστω γλυκύθυμος ὀμμάτων .
sections.translation: mental contrivance . But let delightful sleep be absent from your eyes .

sections.label: Section 707
sections.translation: Attatai ! Attatai !

sections.label: Section 708
sections.translation: What ails you ? Why are you distressed ?

sections.label: Section 709
sections.translation: Wretched man , I am perishing ! The Corinthians , coming out from the bed , are biting me , and devouring my sides , and drinking up my life-blood , and tearing away my flesh , and digging through my vitals , and will annihilate me .

sections.label: Section 716
sections.translation: Do not now be very grievously distressed .

sections.label: Section 717
sections.translation: Why , how , when my money is gone , my complexion gone , my life gone , and my slipper gone ?

sections.label: Section 720
sections.original: καὶ πρὸς τούτοις ἔτι τοῖσι κακοῖς
sections.translation: And furthermore in addition to these evils , with singing the night-watches , I am almost gone myself .

sections.label: Section 723
sections.translation: Ho you ! What are you about ? Are you not meditating ?

sections.label: Section 723b
sections.translation: I ? Yea , by Neptune !

sections.label: Section 724b
sections.translation: And what , pray , have you thought ?

sections.label: Section 725
sections.original: ὑπὸ τῶν κόρεων εἴ μού τι περιλειφθήσεται .
sections.translation: Whether any bit of me will be left by the bugs .

sections.label: Section 726
sections.translation: You will perish most wretchedly .

sections.label: Section 726b
sections.translation: But , my good friend , I have already perished .

sections.label: Section 727
sections.translation: You must not give in , but must wrap yourself up ; for you have to discover a device for abstracting , and a means of cheating .

sections.label: Section 729b
sections.translation: Ah me ! Would , pray , some one would throw over me a swindling contrivance from the sheep-skins .

sections.label: Section 731
sections.original: φέρε νυν ἀθρήσω πρῶτον ὅ τι δρᾷ τουτονί .
sections.translation: Come now ; I will first see this fellow , what he is about . Ho you ! Are you asleep ?

sections.label: Section 732b
sections.original: μὰ τὸν Ἀπόλλω ʼγὼ μὲν οὔ .
sections.translation: No , by Apollo , I am not !
